message.content: Adversaries may exploit email forwarding rules to collect sensitive information, monitor victim activities, and maintain persistent access to emails even after credential resets. This technique involves creating inbox rules to automatically forward emails to internal or external recipients, which can be done through local applications, web interfaces, or command-line interfaces. Adversaries can also hide these rules using the Microsoft Messaging API (MAPI), making them invisible in common email clients and administration tools. Notable groups like Kimsuky, LAPSUS$, and Silent Librarian have used this method to set auto-forwarding rules on compromised accounts. The impact includes unauthorized access to sensitive information and prolonged surveillance of victim communications. Mitigation strategies include regularly auditing email forwarding rules, implementing multi-factor authentication, and monitoring for unusual email rule changes.
